title: Laserpitium transtaganum
intro: Laserpitium transtaganum, also known as Tigan's Laserpitium or Laserpitium, is a plant species in the carrot family (Apiaceae) native to Central Asia. The plant is valued for its health benefits, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and alleviate pain. Laserpitium transtaganum contains a range of bioactive constituents, including sesquiterpenes, coumarins, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Laserpitium transtaganum is commonly prepared as a tincture or infused in tea, and is also used in the production of essential oils and extracts.

The genus Laserpitium itself is a group of flowering plants that are native to Asia.
